up.units: reported per cell/biomass, numeric value in Fig 6A only
mM in medium (10 mM), consumption rate not reported

growth_records.cite: Carolin C. M. Schulte et al. Metabolic control of nitrogen fixation in rhizobium-legume symbioses. Science Advances (2021). DOI:10.1126/sciadv.abh2433. [PMC8324050]
growth_records.snippet: Experimentally determined oxygen consumption rate of R. leguminosarum grown in minimal media with NH4Cl as a nitrogen source and glucose, arabinose, or succinate as the sole carbon source. ... UMS was supplemented ... final concentrations:
growth_records.notes: Experimental condition genuinely measured (O2 consumption rate + generation time, Fig 6A/6C) but absolute numeric values are figure-locked (bar plots) and NOT present in the XML text; no rate could be extracted without inventing. Medium salts base (UMS) cited to ref 61, not tabulated, so medium_composition_stated=false; only C/N supplement concentrations are explicit (glucose 10 mM, NH4Cl 10 mM). Growth at 28 degC, atmospheric 21% O2. mu=ln2/td not computable (td figure-locked).

growth_records.cite: Carolin C. M. Schulte et al. Metabolic control of nitrogen fixation in rhizobium-legume symbioses. Science Advances (2021). DOI:10.1126/sciadv.abh2433. [PMC8324050]
growth_records.snippet: NADH/NAD+ ratio and generation time were measured for cultures grown at 21% oxygen and 1% oxygen.
growth_records.notes: Same strain/medium as glucose-21% record but at 1% O2. Generation time and NADH/NAD+ ratio measured (Fig 6B/6C) but numeric values figure-locked; not extractable from XML. 1% O2 classified microaerophilic.

up.units: 20 mM in medium; consumption rate not reported

growth_records.cite: Carolin C. M. Schulte et al. Metabolic control of nitrogen fixation in rhizobium-legume symbioses. Science Advances (2021). DOI:10.1126/sciadv.abh2433. [PMC8324050]
growth_records.snippet: Catabolism of arabinose, a sugar metabolized via 2-oxoglutarate, induced an oxygen demand similar to growth on succinate. ... arabinose, 20 mM
growth_records.notes: Arabinose (20 mM) sole carbon source, 28 degC, 21% O2. O2 consumption measured (Fig 6A) but figure-locked; text states arabinose O2 demand similar to succinate and higher than glucose but gives no numeric rate.

up.units: 20 mM in medium; consumption rate not reported

growth_records.cite: Carolin C. M. Schulte et al. Metabolic control of nitrogen fixation in rhizobium-legume symbioses. Science Advances (2021). DOI:10.1126/sciadv.abh2433. [PMC8324050]
growth_records.snippet: succinate providing a higher redox value than glucose ... succinate, 20 mM; ... NH4Cl, 10 mM
growth_records.notes: Succinate 20 mM sole C source, 28 degC, 21% O2. Highest O2 consumption and highest NADH/NAD+ of the three carbon sources per text, but numeric values figure-locked (Fig 6A/6B). A slightly higher NADH/NAD+ measured on succinate at 21% vs 1% O2 (attributed to sampling artifact).

growth_records.medium: Universal minimal salts (UMS) medium + ammonium, glucose as sole carbon source

up.method: experimentally determined carbon uptake flux, cited from ref 41 (Cheng G et al. 2017, FEMS Microbiol Lett 364, doi:10.1093/femsle/fnx045); used to constrain the GEM

growth_records.cite: Schulte CCM et al. Genome-Scale Metabolic Modelling of Lifestyle Changes in Rhizobium leguminosarum. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00975-21. [PMC8751395]
growth_records.method: reported (experimental range) + model-predicted; experimental measured mu = 0.131-0.187 h-1 on glucose (cited refs 31, 41, 42). Model-predicted mu after constraining glucose uptake to the experimental value = 0.150 h-1.
growth_records.snippet: Uptake rates of glucose and succinate were constrained to the experimentally determined values of 1.710 mmol h-1 per g cellular dry weight and 3.224 mmol h-1 per g cellular dry weight, respectively (41). ... the predicted growth rates were
growth_records.notes: Rhizobium leguminosarum bv. viciae 3841 grown aerobically at 28C in UMS minimal medium (10 mM glucose + 10 mM NH4Cl + vitamin solution). Experimental glucose uptake rate = 1.710 mmol/gDW/h, taken from ref 41 (Cheng et al. 2017) and used as a model constraint (rate is CITED from another paper, not measured in this study). Experimental growth rate range on glucose = 0.131-0.187 h-1 (cited refs 31, 41, 42). Model-predicted mu after constraining glucose uptake = 0.150 h-1. Doubling time computed from the predicted/validated mu: td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.150 = 4.62 h. No secretion rates reported for the free-living glucose culture. pH not stated in text.

growth_records.medium: Universal minimal salts (UMS) medium + ammonium, succinate as sole carbon source

up.method: experimentally determined carbon uptake flux, cited from ref 41 (Cheng G et al. 2017, FEMS Microbiol Lett 364, doi:10.1093/femsle/fnx045); used to constrain the GEM

growth_records.cite: Schulte CCM et al. Genome-Scale Metabolic Modelling of Lifestyle Changes in Rhizobium leguminosarum. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00975-21. [PMC8751395]
growth_records.method: reported (experimental range) + model-predicted; experimental measured mu = 0.102-0.173 h-1 on succinate (cited refs 31, 41, 42). Model-predicted mu after constraining succinate uptake to the experimental value = 0.149 h-1.
growth_records.snippet: Comparison of metabolic fluxes determined by 13C metabolic flux analysis for Rlv3841 grown on succinate (43) with flux rates predicted by iCS1224. ... Uptake rates of glucose and succinate were constrained to the experimentally determined v
growth_records.notes: Rhizobium leguminosarum bv. viciae 3841 grown aerobically at 28C in UMS minimal medium with succinate as sole carbon source (+ ammonium N). Experimental succinate uptake rate = 3.224 mmol/gDW/h, taken from ref 41 (Cheng et al. 2017) and used as a model constraint (CITED from another paper). Experimental growth rate range on succinate = 0.102-0.173 h-1 (cited refs 31, 41, 42; ref 41 reported substantially slower growth on succinate than glucose). Model-predicted mu after constraining succinate uptake = 0.149 h-1. Doubling time computed: td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.149 = 4.65 h. A 13C-metabolic-flux-analysis dataset of central-carbon fluxes for Rlv3841 grown on succinate exists and was compared against the GEM (cited to ref 43, Terpolilli JJ et al. 2016, J Bacteriol 198:2864-2875, doi:10.1128/JB.00451-16); however the numeric 13C-MFA flux values are presented only in a figure, not in the article text, so mfa_fluxes is left empty rather than fabricating numbers. pH not stated.
